display.text:
name: CI

on:
  push:
    branches: [main]
  pull_request:
    branches: [main]

permissions:
  contents: read

concurrency:
  group: ${{ github.workflow }}-${{ github.event.pull_request.number || github.ref }}
  cancel-in-progress: ${{ github.event_name == 'pull_request' }}

jobs:
  hygiene:
    name: hygiene
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    timeout-minutes: 30
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@df4cb1c069e1874edd31b4311f1884172cec0e10 # v6.0.3
      - uses: ./.github/actions/setup-ci-deps
      - run: mise run ci:lint

  docs:
    name: docs
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    timeout-minutes: 60
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@df4cb1c069e1874edd31b4311f1884172cec0e10 # v6.0.3
      - uses: ./.github/actions/setup-ci-deps
      - run: mise run //docs:build

  variants:
    name: variant / ${{ matrix.mise_env }}
    runs-on: ${{ matrix.runner }}
    timeout-minutes: 120
    env:
      MISE_ENV: ${{ matrix.mise_env }}
    strategy:
      fail-fast: false
      matrix:
        include:
          - mise_env: linux-arm64-egl
            runner: ubuntu-24.04-arm
          - mise_env: linux-arm64-vulkan
            runner: ubuntu-24.04-arm
          - mise_env: linux-x64-egl
            runner: ubuntu-latest
          - mise_env: linux-x64-vulkan
            runner: ubuntu-latest
          - mise_env: macos-arm64-metal
            runner: macos-latest
          - mise_env: macos-arm64-vulkan
            runner: macos-latest
          - mise_env: macos-arm64-egl
            runner: macos-latest
          - mise_env: ios-arm64-metal
            runner: macos-latest
          - mise_env: ios-simulator-arm64-metal
            runner: macos-latest
          - mise_env: windows-x64-vulkan
            runner: windows-2022
          - mise_env: windows-x64-wgl
            runner: windows-2022
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@df4cb1c069e1874edd31b4311f1884172cec0e10 # v6.0.3
      - uses: ./.github/actions/setup-ci-deps

      - run: mise run configure
      - name: Build native library
        if: ${{ startsWith(matrix.mise_env, 'ios-') }}
        run: mise run build
      - name: Test native library
        if: ${{ !startsWith(matrix.mise_env, 'ios-') }}
        run: mise run test

      - name: Build Java FFM binding
        if: ${{ matrix.mise_env != 'macos-arm64-egl' && !startsWith(matrix.mise_env, 'ios-') }}
        run: mise run //bindings/java-ffm:build
      - name: Build Java JNI binding
        if: ${{ matrix.mise_env != 'macos-arm64-egl' && !startsWith(matrix.mise_env, 'ios-') }}
        run: mise run //bindings/java-jni:build
      - name: Test .NET binding
        if: ${{ matrix.mise_env != 'macos-arm64-egl' && !startsWith(matrix.mise_env, 'ios-') }}
        run: mise run //bindings/dotnet:test
      # linux-arm64: no Kotlin/Native host — https://youtrack.jetbrains.com/issue/KT-36871
      # windows: no MSVC target — https://youtrack.jetbrains.com/issue/SKIKO-446
      - name: Test Kotlin/Native binding
        if: ${{ matrix.mise_env != 'macos-arm64-egl' && !startsWith(matrix.mise_env, 'ios-') && !startsWith(matrix.mise_env, 'windows-') && !startsWith(matrix.mise_env, 'linux-arm64-') }}
        run: mise run //bindings/kotlin-native:build
      - name: Test Rust binding
        if: ${{ matrix.mise_env != 'macos-arm64-egl' && !startsWith(matrix.mise_env, 'ios-') }}
        run: mise run //bindings/rust:ci
      - name: Test Swift binding
        if: ${{ matrix.mise_env != 'macos-arm64-egl' && startsWith(matrix.mise_env, 'macos-') }}
        run: mise run //bindings/swift:test
      - name: Test Zig binding
        if: ${{ !startsWith(matrix.mise_env, 'ios-') }}
        run: mise run //bindings/zig:ci

      - name: Build LWJGL example
        if: ${{ contains(matrix.mise_env, 'vulkan') }}
        run: mise run //examples/lwjgl-map:build
      - name: Test Rust example
        if: ${{ matrix.mise_env != 'macos-arm64-egl' && !startsWith(matrix.mise_env, 'ios-') }}
        run: mise run //examples/rust-map:ci
      - name: Build Swift example
        if: ${{ matrix.mise_env == 'macos-arm64-metal' }}
        run: mise run //examples/swift-map:build
      - name: Build Zig example
        if: ${{ matrix.mise_env != 'macos-arm64-egl' && !startsWith(matrix.mise_env, 'ios-') }}
        run: mise run //examples/zig-map:build
      - name: Run Zig readback example
        if: ${{ !startsWith(matrix.mise_env, 'ios-') }}
        run: mise run //examples/zig-readback:run

  required:
    name: ci-required
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    timeout-minutes: 5
    if: ${{ always() }}
    needs:
      - hygiene
      - docs
      - variants
    steps:
      - name: Fail if required jobs failed
        if: ${{ contains(needs.*.result, 'failure') || contains(needs.*.result, 'cancelled') || contains(needs.*.result, 'skipped') }}
        run: |
          echo '${{ toJSON(needs) }}'
          exit 1

      - run: true

part.text:
The CI workflow has a hand-written matrix with variants, plus per-step `if` conditions that encode target support policy (e.g., macos-arm64-egl doesn't support java/dotnet/etc., ios-* build-only, windows no kotlin-native, etc.).

The goal: make target support policy declarative (manifests), generate the checked-in workflow from those manifests, and enforce sync (a check task + CI step, presumably via mise task or hk).

Let me look at the mise.toml, .mise tasks, and the env config files to understand existing structure and tooling.

display.text:
# https://mise.jdx.dev
# Mise is the contributor entrypoint: it pins tools, installs hooks, and runs
# repository tasks.

experimental_monorepo_root = true

[settings]
lockfile = true
lockfile_platforms = ["linux-x64", "linux-arm64", "macos-arm64", "windows-x64"]
pin = true
experimental = true
python.uv_venv_auto = "create|source"
windows_default_file_shell_args = '"C:/Program Files/Git/bin/bash.exe" -e -c "source ""$MLN_FFI_REPO_ROOT/.mise/bin/windows-msvc-path.sh""; exec ""C:/Program Files/Git/bin/bash.exe"" ""$@""" _'
windows_default_inline_shell_args = '"C:/Program Files/Git/bin/bash.exe" -e -c "source ""$MLN_FFI_REPO_ROOT/.mise/bin/windows-msvc-path.sh""; eval ""$1""" _'
unix_default_file_shell_args = "bash"
unix_default_inline_shell_args = "bash -e -lc"
# On Windows, the Microsoft Store Python alias in WindowsApps can appear before
# mise-managed Python. Keep mise tool paths first so file tasks run pinned tools.
activate_aggressive = true

[monorepo]
config_roots = [".devcontainer", "bindings/*", "examples/*", "docs"]

[tools]
# General tooling
"aqua:jdx/usage" = "3.3.0" # CLI completion for mise tasks
"aqua:jdx/hk" = "1.45.0" # Linter orchestration
"aqua:apple/pkl" = "0.31.0" # hk config dependency
"aqua:dprint/dprint" = "0.53.2" # Formatter orchestration
"github:sargunv/jvl" = "2026.05.03" # JSON validator
"aqua:rhysd/actionlint" = "1.7.8" # GH Actions linter
"aqua:koalaman/shellcheck" = "0.11.0" # Shell script linter (used by actionlint)

# C/C++ ecosystem
"github:prefix-dev/pixi" = "0.68.1"
"aqua:Kitware/CMake" = "4.3.3"
"aqua:ninja-build/ninja" = "1.13.2"
"conda:clang-format" = "22.1.7"
"conda:clang-tools" = "22.1.7"
"conda:doxygen" = "1.13.2"
"conda:glslang" = "16.3.0"
"conda:pkg-config" = "0.29.2"
"conda:vulkan-tools" = "1.4.341.0"
"github:cmakefmt/cmakefmt" = "1.3.0"
"aqua:XcodesOrg/xcodes" = { version = "1.6.2", os = ["macos"] }

# Node.js ecosystem
"core:node" = "24.11.0"
"aqua:pnpm/pnpm" = "10.33.2"

# Java/Kotlin ecosystem
"core:java" = "temurin-25.0.3+9.0.LTS"
"aqua:facebook/ktfmt" = "0.62"
"github:google/google-java-format" = "1.35.0"

# Python ecosystem
"core:python" = "3.14.4"
"aqua:astral-sh/uv" = "0.11.11"

# Swift ecosystem
"core:swift" = { version = "6.3.1", os = ["macos"] }
"github:nicklockwood/SwiftFormat" = "0.61.1"

# Other ecosystems
"core:rust" = { version = "1.95.0", profile = "minimal", components = "rustfmt,clippy" }
"core:zig" = "0.16.0"
"core:go" = "1.26.3"
"core:dotnet" = "10.0.203"

[env]
UV_PYTHON = { value = "{{ tools.python.path }}", tools = true }
MLN_FFI_REPO_ROOT = "{{config_root}}"
MLN_FFI_VARIANT = { required = true }
MLN_FFI_TARGET_TRIPLE = { required = true }
MLN_FFI_RENDER_BACKEND = { required = true }
MLN_FFI_BUILD_DIR = { required = true }
MLN_FFI_CMAKE_BUILD_TYPE = "{{env.MLN_FFI_CMAKE_BUILD_TYPE | default(value=\"RelWithDebInfo\")}}"
MLN_FFI_INCLUDE_DIR = "{{config_root}}/include"
MLN_FFI_VULKAN_INCLUDE_DIR = "{{config_root}}/third_party/maplibre-native/vendor/Vulkan-Headers/include"
MLN_FFI_DEPENDENCY_INCLUDE_DIR = "{{config_root}}/.pixi/envs/default/include"
MLN_FFI_DEPENDENCY_LIBRARY_DIR = "{{config_root}}/.pixi/envs/default/lib"
MLN_FFI_ZIG_TARGET = { required = true }
MLN_FFI_ZIG_BUILD_ARGS = { required = true }
MLN_FFI_TEST_TIMEOUT = "{{env.MLN_FFI_TEST_TIMEOUT | default(value=\"30s\")}}"
CMAKE_PREFIX_PATH = "{{config_root}}/.pixi/envs/default"
PKG_CONFIG_PATH = "{{config_root}}/.pixi/envs/default/lib/pkgconfig"
LIBCLANG_PATH = "{{config_root}}/.pixi/envs/default/lib"

[hooks]
postinstall = [
  { task = "sync-submodules" },
  "hk install --mise --quiet",
  "pixi install --locked --quiet",
  # conda-forge ships libclang-13.dll, but clang-sys on Windows searches for libclang.dll.
  # https://github.com/KyleMayes/clang-sys/issues/213
  # https://github.com/conda-forge/llvmdev-feedstock/issues/376
  '{% if os() == "windows" %}libclang_bin="$(dirname "$MLN_FFI_DEPENDENCY_LIBRARY_DIR")/bin"; cp "$libclang_bin/libclang-13.dll" "$libclang_bin/libclang.dll"{% endif %}',
  "uv sync --locked --quiet",
  "pnpm install --frozen-lockfile --loglevel warn",
  "dotnet tool restore --tool-manifest dotnet-tools.json --verbosity quiet",
  "dotnet tool restore --tool-manifest bindings/dotnet/dotnet-tools.json --verbosity quiet",
  # Astro type stubs for vp typeCheck on docs sources (.astro/ is gitignored).
  "pnpm --filter docs exec astro sync",
]

[tasks.configure]
depends = ["//:deps"]
run = [
  '''
  cmake_args=(
    -S .
    -B "$MLN_FFI_BUILD_DIR"
    -G Ninja
    -DCMAKE_BUILD_TYPE="$MLN_FFI_CMAKE_BUILD_TYPE"
    -DMLN_FFI_RENDER_BACKEND="$MLN_FFI_RENDER_BACKEND"
    -DMLN_FFI_OPENGL_CONTEXT_PROVIDER="$MLN_FFI_OPENGL_CONTEXT_PROVIDER"
    -DMLN_FFI_EGL_ROOT="$MLN_FFI_EGL_ROOT"
  )

  while IFS= read -r cmake_arg; do
    [[ -n "$cmake_arg" ]] && cmake_args+=("$cmake_arg")
  done <<< "${MLN_FFI_CMAKE_ARGS:-}"

  cmake "${cmake_args[@]}"
  ''',
  '''
  if [[ -f "$MLN_FFI_BUILD_DIR/compile_commands.json" ]]; then
    mkdir -p build/clangd
    compile_commands_link="build/clangd/compile_commands.json"
    compile_commands_tmp="${compile_commands_link}.tmp"
    rm -f "$compile_commands_tmp"
    ln -s "$MLN_FFI_BUILD_DIR/compile_commands.json" "$compile_commands_tmp"
    mv -f "$compile_commands_tmp" "$compile_commands_link"
  fi
  ''',
]

[tasks.build]
depends = ["//:configure"]
run = '''
build_args=(
  --build "$MLN_FFI_BUILD_DIR"
  --parallel
)

cmake "${build_args[@]}"'''

[tasks.clean]
run = "rm -rf \"$MLN_FFI_BUILD_DIR\""

[tasks.deps]
description = "Install variant-specific managed dependencies."
run = '''
if [[ "$MLN_FFI_TARGET_TRIPLE" == "macos-arm64" ]]; then
  mise run deps:angle
fi
'''

[tasks.test]
depends = ["//:build"]
run = '''
(
  cd src/c_api/tests/zig
  zig build test {{ env.MLN_FFI_ZIG_BUILD_ARGS }} --test-timeout "$MLN_FFI_TEST_TIMEOUT" --summary all --verbose
)
(
  cd bindings/zig
  zig build test {{ env.MLN_FFI_ZIG_BUILD_ARGS }} --test-timeout "$MLN_FFI_TEST_TIMEOUT" --summary all --verbose
)
'''

[tasks.check]
run = "hk check --all"

[tasks.fix]
run = "hk fix --all"

[tasks.actions-up]
description = "Update and pin GitHub Actions steps."
run = "pnpm dlx actions-up"

[tasks._ktfmt]
description = "Called by dprint.jsonc"
hide = true
run = 'java -jar {{ tools["aqua:facebook/ktfmt"].path }}/ktfmt'
# ktfmt on windows doesn't work in git bash for some reason
shell = '{% if os() == "windows" %}cmd /c{% else %}sh -c -o errexit{% endif %}'

[tasks."ci:lint"]
run = [
  # Warm process-plugin cache before hk runs parallel dprint batches.
  "dprint output-resolved-config > /dev/null",
  "hk fix --all",
  "git update-index -q --refresh",
  # Lockfile drift is common across different mise builds
  "git diff --exit-code -- . ':(exclude)mise.lock'",
  "git diff --cached --exit-code -- . ':(exclude)mise.lock'",
]

[tasks."ci:vulkan-info"]
description = "Print the active Vulkan loader and driver summary."
run = "vulkaninfo --summary"

setup-ci-deps
name: Setup CI deps
description: Install sccache and mise toolchain

runs:
  using: composite
  steps:
    - name: Enable Git long paths
      if: ${{ runner.os == 'Windows' }}
      shell: pwsh
      run: git config --global core.longpaths true

    - name: Cache MapLibre Native source
      uses: actions/cache@27d5ce7f107fe9357f9df03efb73ab90386fccae # v5.0.5
      with:
        path: |
          third_party/maplibre-native
          .git/modules/third_party/maplibre-native
        key: mln-src-v2-${{ hashFiles('.gitmodules', 'cmake/mln_version.cmake') }}

    - name: Install sccache
      uses: mozilla-actions/sccache-action@9e7fa8a12102821edf02ca5dbea1acd0f89a2696 # v0.0.10

    - name: Use Linux software graphics drivers
      if: ${{ runner.os == 'Linux' }}
      shell: bash
      run: |
        sudo apt-get update
        sudo apt-get install --no-install-recommends --yes \
          libegl-mesa0 \
          libgl1-mesa-dri \
          libvulkan1 \
          mesa-utils \
          mesa-utils-extra \
          mesa-vulkan-drivers
        {
          echo "EGL_PLATFORM=surfaceless"
          echo "LIBGL_ALWAYS_SOFTWARE=true"
        } >> "$GITHUB_ENV"

    - name: Use Windows software graphics drivers
      if: ${{ runner.os == 'Windows' }}
      shell: pwsh
      run: |
        $mesaVersion = "26.0.8"
        $mesaArchive = Join-Path $env:RUNNER_TEMP "mesa3d.7z"
        $mesaRoot = Join-Path $env:RUNNER_TEMP "mesa3d"
        $mesaUrl = "https://github.com/pal1000/mesa-dist-win/releases/download/$mesaVersion/mesa3d-$mesaVersion-release-msvc.7z"

        Invoke-WebRequest -Uri $mesaUrl -OutFile $mesaArchive
        $expectedHash = "A438C26C2752726916E455F9AD121F8A7E3CFECF8626251ABF8E5B3E129D8497"
        $actualHash = (Get-FileHash $mesaArchive -Algorithm SHA256).Hash
        if ($actualHash -ne $expectedHash) {
          throw "Mesa archive hash mismatch: expected $expectedHash, got $actualHash"
        }
        7z x $mesaArchive "-o$mesaRoot" -y | Out-Null

        $mesaWglDriver = Get-ChildItem $mesaRoot -Recurse -Filter "libgallium_wgl.dll" |
          Where-Object { $_.FullName -match "x64" } |
          Select-Object -First 1
        if (-not $mesaWglDriver) {
          throw "Missing Mesa WGL driver in $mesaArchive"
        }

        $driverManifest = Get-ChildItem $mesaRoot -Recurse -Filter "lvp_icd.x86_64.json" |
          Select-Object -First 1
        if (-not $driverManifest) {
          throw "Missing lavapipe Vulkan ICD manifest in $mesaArchive"
        }

        $mesaBin = $mesaWglDriver.Directory.FullName
        $mesaWglSystemDriver = Join-Path $env:WINDIR "System32\mesadrv.dll"
        Copy-Item $mesaWglDriver.FullName $mesaWglSystemDriver -Force

        $openglDriverKey = "HKLM:\SOFTWARE\Microsoft\Windows NT\CurrentVersion\OpenGLDrivers\MSOGL"
        New-Item -Path $openglDriverKey -Force | Out-Null
        New-ItemProperty -Path $openglDriverKey -Name DLL -PropertyType String -Value "mesadrv.dll" -Force | Out-Null
        New-ItemProperty -Path $openglDriverKey -Name DriverVersion -PropertyType DWord -Value 1 -Force | Out-Null
        New-ItemProperty -Path $openglDriverKey -Name Flags -PropertyType DWord -Value 1 -Force | Out-Null
        New-ItemProperty -Path $openglDriverKey -Name Version -PropertyType DWord -Value 2 -Force | Out-Null

        $driverKey = "HKLM:\SOFTWARE\Khronos\Vulkan\Drivers"
        New-Item -Path $driverKey -Force | Out-Null
        New-ItemProperty -Path $driverKey -Name $driverManifest.FullName -PropertyType DWord -Value 0 -Force | Out-Null

        "GALLIUM_DRIVER=llvmpipe" >> $env:GITHUB_ENV
        "Path=$mesaBin;$env:Path" >> $env:GITHUB_ENV

    - name: Cache Cargo packages
      uses: actions/cache@27d5ce7f107fe9357f9df03efb73ab90386fccae # v5.0.5
      with:
        path: |
          ~/.cargo/registry/index/
          ~/.cargo/registry/cache/
          ~/.cargo/git/db/
        key: ${{ runner.os }}-${{ runner.arch }}-cargo-registry-v1-${{ hashFiles('**/Cargo.lock') }}

    - name: Cache Gradle
      uses: actions/cache@27d5ce7f107fe9357f9df03efb73ab90386fccae # v5.0.5
      with:
        path: |
          ~/.gradle/caches
          ~/.gradle/wrapper
        key: ${{ runner.os }}-${{ runner.arch }}-gradle-${{ hashFiles('**/*.gradle*', '**/gradle-wrapper.properties') }}
        restore-keys: |
          ${{ runner.os }}-${{ runner.arch }}-gradle-

    - name: Trim Windows PATH before mise activation
      if: ${{ runner.os == 'Windows' }}
      shell: pwsh
      run: |
        $workspaceDrive = Split-Path -Qualifier $env:GITHUB_WORKSPACE
        # Git Bash maps /tmp to the checkout drive; create it before mise hooks run.
        New-Item -ItemType Directory -Force -Path (Join-Path $workspaceDrive "tmp") | Out-Null

        $paths = @(
          if ($env:SCCACHE_PATH) { Split-Path -Parent $env:SCCACHE_PATH }
          if ($env:Path) { $env:Path -split ';' }
          "C:\Program Files\Git\bin",
          "C:\Program Files\Git\usr\bin",
          "$env:SystemRoot\System32",
          "$env:SystemRoot",
          "$env:SystemRoot\System32\Wbem",
          "$env:SystemRoot\System32\WindowsPowerShell\v1.0",
          "C:\Program Files\PowerShell\7",
          "C:\Program Files\Git\cmd"
        ) | Where-Object { $_ -and (Test-Path $_) }

        "Path=$($paths -join ';')" >> $env:GITHUB_ENV

        $gradleTemp = $env:RUNNER_TEMP -replace '\\', '/'
        $gradleOptions = "-Djava.io.tmpdir=$gradleTemp"
        if ($env:GRADLE_OPTS) {
          $gradleOptions = "$gradleOptions $env:GRADLE_OPTS"
        }
        "GRADLE_OPTS=$gradleOptions" >> $env:GITHUB_ENV

    - name: Install mise toolchain
      uses: jdx/mise-action@1648a7812b9aeae629881980618f079932869151 # v4.0.1
      with:
        cache: true
        cache_key_prefix: mise-v2
        install: true

    - name: Wire sccache into CMake
      shell: bash
      run: |
        {
          echo "SCCACHE_GHA_ENABLED=true"
          echo "SCCACHE_GHA_VERSION=${MLN_FFI_TARGET_TRIPLE}"
          echo "SCCACHE_BASEDIRS=${GITHUB_WORKSPACE}"
          echo "CMAKE_C_COMPILER_LAUNCHER=sccache"
          echo "CMAKE_CXX_COMPILER_LAUNCHER=sccache"
        } >> "$GITHUB_ENV"

    - name: Diagnose EGL setup
      if: ${{ runner.os == 'Linux' }}
      shell: bash
      run: |
        mise exec -- bash -euo pipefail -c '
          env | sort | grep -E "^(EGL|LIBGL|MESA|LD_LIBRARY_PATH|__EGL|MLN_FFI|VK_)" || true
          command -v eglinfo
          EGL_LOG_LEVEL=debug LIBGL_DEBUG=verbose LD_DEBUG=libs eglinfo || true
        '

    - name: Diagnose Vulkan setup
      if: ${{ contains(env.MISE_ENV, 'vulkan') }}
      shell: bash
      run: mise run ci:vulkan-info

    # The mise action may restore Rust metadata without all rustup-managed
    # component files. Repair the toolchain only when rustup cannot add the
    # configured components or the component binaries still cannot run.
    - name: Ensure Rust components
      shell: bash
      run: |
        ensure_rust_components() {
          mise exec -- rustup component add rustfmt clippy &&
            mise exec -- cargo fmt --version &&
            mise exec -- cargo clippy --version
        }

        if ! ensure_rust_components; then
          mise install --force rust
          ensure_rust_components
        fi
// hk orchestrates checks for pre-commit, `mise run check`, and `mise run fix`.
// Tools used here must be installed by `mise install`.

amends "package://github.com/jdx/hk/releases/download/v1.45.0/hk@1.45.0#/Config.pkl"

import "package://github.com/jdx/hk/releases/download/v1.45.0/hk@1.45.0#/Builtins.pkl"

local lintSteps = new Mapping<String, Step> {
  ["dprint"] = (Builtins.dprint) {
    // the default batch=false hits the cmd length limit on windows
    batch = true
  }
  ["actionlint"] {
    glob = List(".github/workflows/*.yml", ".github/workflows/*.yaml")
    check = "actionlint {{files}}"
  }
  ["jvl"] {
    glob = List("**/*.json", "**/*.jsonc")
    check = "jvl check {{files}}"
  }
  // Mirrors Builtins.`vp-check` (available in hk 1.46+). dprint owns formatting.
  ["vp-check"] {
    glob = List(
      "**/*.js",
      "**/*.mjs",
      "**/*.cjs",
      "**/*.ts",
      "**/*.mts",
      "**/*.cts",
      "**/*.jsx",
      "**/*.tsx",
      "**/*.vue",
      "**/*.svelte",
      "**/*.astro",
    )
    exclude = List("third_party/**")
    check = "pnpm exec vp check --no-fmt {{files}}"
    fix = "pnpm exec vp check --no-fmt --fix {{files}}"
  }
  ["ruff"] {
    glob = List("**/*.py")
    exclude = List("third_party/**")
    check = "uv run ruff check {{files}} && uv run ruff format --check {{files}}"
    fix = "uv run ruff check --fix {{files}} && uv run ruff format {{files}}"
  }
  ["ty"] {
    glob = List("**/*.py")
    exclude = List("third_party/**")
    check = "uv run ty check --error-on-warning .mise"
  }
}

hooks {
  ["pre-commit"] {
    fix = true
    stash = "git"
    steps {
      ...lintSteps
    }
  }
  ["check"] {
    steps {
      ...lintSteps
    }
  }
  ["fix"] {
    fix = true
    steps {
      ...lintSteps
    }
  }
}

          env | sort | grep -E "^(EGL|LIBGL|MESA|LD_LIBRARY_PATH|__EGL|MLN_FFI|VK_)" || true
          command -v eglinfo
          EGL_LOG_LEVEL=debug LIBGL_DEBUG=verbose LD_DEBUG=libs eglinfo || true
        '

    - name: Diagnose Vulkan setup
      if: ${{ contains(env.MISE_ENV, 'vulkan') }}
      shell: bash
      run: mise run ci:vulkan-info

    # The mise action may restore Rust metadata without all rustup-managed
    # component files. Repair the toolchain only when rustup cannot add the
    # configured components or the component binaries still cannot run.
    - name: Ensure Rust components
      shell: bash
      run: |
        ensure_rust_components() {
          mise exec -- rustup component add rustfmt clippy &&
            mise exec -- cargo fmt --version &&
            mise exec -- cargo clippy --version
        }

        if ! ensure_rust_components; then
          mise install --force rust
          ensure_rust_components
        fi
// hk orchestrates checks for pre-commit, `mise run check`, and `mise run fix`.
// Tools used here must be installed by `mise install`.

amends "package://github.com/jdx/hk/releases/download/v1.45.0/hk@1.45.0#/Config.pkl"

import "package://github.com/jdx/hk/releases/download/v1.45.0/hk@1.45.0#/Builtins.pkl"

local lintSteps = new Mapping<String, Step> {
  ["dprint"] = (Builtins.dprint) {
    // the default batch=false hits the cmd length limit on windows
    batch = true
  }
  ["actionlint"] {
    glob = List(".github/workflows/*.yml", ".github/workflows/*.yaml")
    check = "actionlint {{files}}"
  }
  ["jvl"] {
    glob = List("**/*.json", "**/*.jsonc")
    check = "jvl check {{files}}"
  }
  // Mirrors Builtins.`vp-check` (available in hk 1.46+). dprint owns formatting.
  ["vp-check"] {
    glob = List(
      "**/*.js",
      "**/*.mjs",
      "**/*.cjs",
      "**/*.ts",
      "**/*.mts",
      "**/*.cts",
      "**/*.jsx",
      "**/*.tsx",
      "**/*.vue",
      "**/*.svelte",
      "**/*.astro",
    )
    exclude = List("third_party/**")
    check = "pnpm exec vp check --no-fmt {{files}}"
    fix = "pnpm exec vp check --no-fmt --fix {{files}}"
  }
  ["ruff"] {
    glob = List("**/*.py")
    exclude = List("third_party/**")
    check = "uv run ruff check {{files}} && uv run ruff format --check {{files}}"
    fix = "uv run ruff check --fix {{files}} && uv run ruff format {{files}}"
  }
  ["ty"] {
    glob = List("**/*.py")
    exclude = List("third_party/**")
    check = "uv run ty check --error-on-warning .mise"
  }
}

hooks {
  ["pre-commit"] {
    fix = true
    stash = "git"
    steps {
      ...lintSteps
    }
  }
  ["check"] {
    steps {
      ...lintSteps
    }
  }
  ["fix"] {
    fix = true
    steps {
      ...lintSteps
    }
  }
}

part.text:
Now I understand the setup. The task:

1. Make CI target support policy declarative — manifests describing each variant's support (e.g., which bindings/examples run).
2. Generate the checked-in workflow from manifests (a generator script).
3. Enforce it stays in sync (a check task wired into ci:lint or hygiene job).
4. Preserve current CI behavior unless clearly duplicated policy.
5. Test the generator (add tests).
6. Commit the finished change.

There's a Python ecosystem available (uv, ruff, ty — the `ty` hook checks `.mise` dir!). Note `check = "uv run ty check --error-on-warning .mise"` — interesting: type checking for Python in `.mise`. So Python scripts in `.mise` directory are type-checked. That's a hint that the generator should live in `.mise` as Python, run via `uv run`.

Let me check pyproject.toml and .venv.

part.text:
Now I understand the layout. Design plan:

**Manifests**: One TOML manifest per CI variant, or a single manifest file? The task says "generate the checked-in workflow from manifests". I'll create `ci/variants/*.toml` or `.github/ci/*.toml`. Given the repo structure, maybe put manifests in `.mise/ci/variants/*.toml`? Actually the generator will be Python in `.mise` (since `ty` checks `.mise`). Hmm, `ty check .mise` — that's the type check path. So Python files in `.mise` get type-checked. That suggests generator script lives in `.mise` somewhere, e.g. `.mise/tasks/` or a new `.mise/scripts/`.

Let me design:

- **Manifests**: `ci/targets/*.toml` — one per variant, e.g. `linux-x64-egl.toml`:

```toml
mise_env = "linux-x64-egl"
runner = "ubuntu-latest"

# What runs on this target
[jobs]
test = true          # false => build-only (ios)
...
```

Better: declarative capability flags:

```toml
runner = "ubuntu-latest"
build_only = false  # ios: no display -> build only

[steps.bindings]
java = true
dotnet = true
kotlin_native = true
rust = true
swift = false
zig = true

[steps.examples]
lwjgl = true
rust = true
swift = false
zig = true
zig_readback = true
```

Current policy derived from the `if` conditions:

Common conditions:
- `!startsWith(mise_env, 'ios-')` → native `test` vs `build`; java-ffm, java-jni, dotnet, kotlin-native, rust binding, rust example, zig example, zig-readback; zig binding runs even on ios though? Line 99-101: Test Zig binding `if: !startsWith ios`. Wait line 100: `if: ${{ !startsWith(matrix.mise_env, 'ios-') }}` — zig binding also excluded on ios. And zig-readback excluded on ios. Actually every binding/example step except LWJGL (vulkan) and Swift (macos) excludes ios. Only native build runs on ios.

- `matrix.mise_env != 'macos-arm64-egl'` → excludes java, dotnet, kotlin-native, rust binding, swift (swift requires macos- prefix AND != egl), rust example, zig example. But zig binding and zig-readback DO run on macos-arm64-egl. Hmm interesting. So macos-arm64-egl excludes most things — probably because ANGLE lacks something (JNI AWT? maybe headless). But Zig binding/readback work.

- kotlin-native additionally excluded on windows-* and linux-arm64-*.

- swift: only `macos-* && != macos-arm64-egl` → macos-arm64-metal, macos-arm64-vulkan.

- swift example: only `macos-arm64-metal`.

- lwjgl example: `contains(mise_env, 'vulkan')` → all vulkan variants: linux-arm64-vulkan, linux-x64-vulkan, macos-arm64-vulkan, windows-x64-vulkan.

So the declarative manifest per variant:

```toml
# ci/targets/linux-x64-vulkan.toml
runner = "ubuntu-latest"
run_tests = true  # build native and run tests; false = build-only (ios)

[bindings]
java = true
dotnet = true
kotlin-native = true
rust = true
swift = false
zig = true

[examples]
lwjgl = true
rust = true
swift = false
zig = true
zig-readback = true
```

And the generator produces the matrix + step if-conditions. Also keep comments (kotlin-native links). The comments in the YAML: "linux-arm64: no Kotlin/Native host — ...; windows: no MSVC target — ...". Those could be stored in the manifest as `reason`/notes and re-emitted as comments.

Where to put things:
- Manifests: `ci/targets/*.toml`? Or `.github/ci/`? I'll go with `ci/targets/` at repo root... Hmm. Actually given `.mise` is where contributor tooling lives and `ty` is configured to check `.mise`, putting the generator in `.mise` makes it type-checked. Manifests location: maybe `.mise/ci/targets/*.toml`. Hmm, but "manifests" describing CI policy — I'd place at `ci/` root directory for visibility. Let me keep simple:

- `ci/targets/*.toml` — one manifest per variant.
- `ci/generate_workflow.py` — generator (stdlib only: tomllib in py3.14). Wait, ruff checks `**/*.py` and ty checks only `.mise`. To get type-checking, put generator under `.mise`? The ty hook: `uv run ty check --error-on-warning .mise`. Ruff lints all py files. I could extend ty to also check `ci/`. Simpler: put the generator in `.mise/tasks/` as a mise file task? Mise file tasks can be Python with a shebang. But then ty would check it (it's in .mise). Hmm, but mise file tasks with `.py` extension... they'd be picked up by ruff too. That's fine.

Actually simpler and cleaner: a Python script at `.mise/scripts/generate_ci_workflow.py` plus a mise task `ci:generate-workflow` and `ci:check-workflow` (or a single task with --check). Tests for the generator: Python unittest, run via `uv run python -m unittest`. Where do tests run? Add a mise task `ci:test-workflow-generator`? Or fold tests into the check task. Let me think about "test the generator" — write unit tests that e.g.:
- every `.mise/config.<env>.toml` variant has a manifest and vice versa (sync between mise envs and manifests)
- rendering is deterministic / matches checked-in workflow
- manifests load and validate (unknown keys rejected)
- rendered workflow reproduces the current behavior (the checked-in file matches generated output)

Enforcement: add a step in the `hygiene` job / `ci:lint` task that runs `python .mise/scripts/generate_ci_workflow.py --check` (fails if workflow drifted). Also validate manifests cover all mise env configs. And tests run... maybe in hygiene too. Keep contributor workflow simple: `mise run ci:workflow` to regenerate; check runs in ci:lint.

Let me now decide manifest schema more concretely. Policy knobs in current workflow:

1. runner
2. build-only vs test (ios = build-only)
3. java (ffm + jni together — same condition)
4. dotnet
5. kotlin-native (+ reason comments)
6. rust binding
7. swift binding
8. zig binding (runs everywhere except ios)
9. examples: lwjgl (vulkan), rust, swift (macos-arm64-metal only), zig, zig-readback

Note zig binding condition is exactly `!ios` — same as run_tests. And zig-readback too. So those could just be tied to run_tests... but "preserve current behavior unless clearly duplicated policy". The step-level conditions are the policy. I'll model each step group as boolean capability with optional `reason` (emitted as comment when false... or when restricted).

Wait — should reasons be emitted only when false? The existing comments document why kotlin-native is skipped on some platforms. In generated YAML, a comment above the step listing the platforms where it's disabled would be generated from manifests. Let me design the manifest:

```toml
# CI target: linux-x64 with the EGL/OpenGL backend.
runner = "ubuntu-latest"

# When true, run the full native test suite; when false, build the native
# library only (e.g. iOS, which has no display to run headless tests).
test = true

# Binding and example steps enabled on this target. A step listed with a
# `reason` is skipped and the reason is emitted into the workflow.
[steps]
java-ffm = true
java-jni = true
dotnet = true
kotlin-native = true
rust = true
swift = false
zig = true
lwjgl-example = true
rust-example = true
swift-example = false
zig-example = true
zig-readback-example = true
```

Hmm, TOML with mixed bool/string is ugly. Better:

```toml
[steps.kotin-native]
enabled = false
reason = "no Kotlin/Native linux-arm64 host — https://youtrack.jetbrains.com/issue/KT-36871"
```

Verbose. Alternative: enabled steps = list:

```toml
steps = ["java-ffm", "java-jni", "dotnet", "kotlin-native", "rust", "zig", "lwjgl", "rust-example", "zig-example", "zig-readback"]
```

And a global steps definition in a shared manifest (`ci/steps.toml`?) that defines each step: name, run command, default condition... Hmm, that could over-engineer. The step definitions (names, mise tasks, ordering) are workflow structure, not policy — they can stay in the generator template. The policy is per-target: runner, test mode, and per-step enablement + skip reasons.

I think a good structure:

`ci/targets/_steps.toml`? No — keep generator self-contained with step table; manifests per target:

```toml
# linux-arm64-egl.toml
runner = "ubuntu-24.04-arm"

[steps]
kotlin-native = { enabled = false, reason = "no Kotlin/Native host for linux-arm64 — https://youtrack.jetbrains.com/issue/KT-36871" }
```

With defaults defined in the generator: each step has a default enablement derived from variant properties? E.g. swift default = enabled iff platform is macos && backend != egl. Hmm, that's policy encoded in the generator — is that OK? "Make it declarative" — the policy should be in manifests. But per-step booleans for all 12 steps × 11 variants is fine and fully explicit. Let me compute the full matrix:

Steps and current conditions:

| step | condition |
|---|---|
| java-ffm build | !egl-macos && !ios |
| java-jni build | !egl-macos && !ios |
| dotnet test | !egl-macos && !ios |
| kotlin-native build | !egl-macos && !ios && !windows && !linux-arm64 |
| rust test | !egl-macos && !ios |
| swift test | macos && !egl-macos → metal, vulkan |
| zig ci | !ios |
| lwjgl build | vulkan |
| rust example ci | !egl-macos && !ios |
| swift example build | macos-arm64-metal only |
| zig example build | !egl-macos && !ios |
| zig-readback run | !ios |

Per-variant table (variants: linux-arm64-egl, linux-arm64-vulkan, linux-x64-egl, linux-x64-vulkan, macos-arm64-metal, macos-arm64-vulkan, macos-arm64-egl, ios-arm64-metal, ios-simulator-arm64-metal, windows-x64-vulkan, windows-x64-wgl):

- linux-arm64-egl: java✓ dotnet✓ kotlin✗ rust✓ swift✗ zig✓ lwjgl✗ rust-ex✓ swift-ex✗ zig-ex✓ readback✓ test✓
- linux-arm64-vulkan: same + lwjgl✓
- linux-x64-egl: kotlin✓, lwjgl✗
- linux-x64-vulkan: kotlin✓, lwjgl✓
- macos-arm64-metal: java✓ dotnet✓ kotlin✓ rust✓ swift✓ zig✓ lwjgl✗ rust-ex✓ swift-ex✓ zig-ex✓ readback✓
- macos-arm64-vulkan: same but swift-ex✗, lwjgl✓
- macos-arm64-egl: java✗ dotnet✗ kotlin✗ rust✗ swift✗ zig✓ lwjgl✗ rust-ex✗ swift-ex✗ zig-ex✗ readback✓
- ios-arm64-metal: all ✗, build-only
- ios-simulator-arm64-metal: all ✗, build-only
- windows-x64-vulkan: java✓ dotnet✓ kotlin✗ rust✓ swift✗ zig✓ lwjgl✓ rust-ex✓ zig-ex✓ readback✓
- windows-x64-wgl: same minus lwjgl

Manifest per variant with explicit steps map. Reasons for disabled steps:
- kotlin-native on linux-arm64: "no Kotlin/Native host — KT-36871"
- kotlin-native on windows: "no MSVC target — SKIKO-446"
- macos-arm64-egl: many disabled... no reasons given in current workflow except it excludes. I'll allow optional reason; only kotlin-native has documented reasons.

Generator emits the `if:` conditions. To preserve the workflow YAML's conditions exactly (so actionlint etc. fine and behavior identical), the generator should map the per-step enabled set to a condition expression:

For each step, given the set of variants where it's enabled, generate minimal condition? That could produce different-but-equivalent expressions. Simpler and more robust: generate condition from exclusion sets using positive matchers. E.g. for kotlin-native: enabled variants = {linux-x64-egl, linux-x64-vulkan, macos-arm64-metal, macos-arm64-vulkan}. Condition could be `contains('linux-x64-egl,linux-x64-vulkan,macos-arm64-metal,macos-arm64-vulkan', matrix.mise_env)` — hmm ugly.

Alternative: generate `if` per step as explicit inclusion: `${{ contains(fromJSON('["linux-x64-egl",...]'), matrix.mise_env) }}`. GitHub expressions support fromJSON with arrays and contains. That's declarative and avoids the generator needing to invent minimal boolean expressions over prefixes. But it changes the workflow text from hand-written prefix conditions to membership lists — behavior preserved, arguably clearer. But `contains` with arrays works in `if`. Yes: `contains(fromJSON('["a","b"]'), matrix.mise_env)`.

Hmm, but is that nicer? The existing prefix conditions are compact. A generator that synthesizes minimal prefix expressions is complex. Membership-list approach is straightforward, deterministic, and self-documenting (you can see exactly which targets run each step). I'll go with that.

Also the ios build-only: `test` boolean in manifest → native step becomes `mise run build` when test=false, `mise run test` when true. Generate two steps as now: "Build native library" if any build-only variants, "Test native library" for others.

Workflow skeleton (header, hygiene, docs, required jobs) stays fixed in the template within the generator. The generator writes the whole `ci.yml`. Header comment: "This file is generated by ... do not edit; edit ci/targets/*.toml".

Sync enforcement:
1. `mise run ci:workflow --check`? Better two tasks: `ci:gen-workflow` and integrate check into `ci:lint`. Add to `[tasks."ci:lint"]` a run line: `uv run .mise/scripts/gen_ci_workflow.py --check`. And a contributor task `[tasks."ci:workflow"]` that regenerates. Also validation that manifests ↔ .mise/config.*.toml variants match (in --check mode and tests).

Wait: ci:lint runs `hk fix --all` first then checks git diff — if the workflow is out of sync, should fix regenerate it? That would be nice: hook the generator into hk fix for the workflow file? Could add an hk step that runs the generator on fix of ci/targets/*.toml or .github/workflows/ci.yml. Hmm, "Keep normal contributor workflow simple". The simplest: hk step `ci-workflow` with glob `ci/targets/*.toml`, fix = generate + stage, check = verify in sync. Since ci:lint runs hk fix and then git diff --exit-code, drift would fail CI automatically if hk regenerates... wait no — if hk fix regenerates the workflow, git diff would be empty and CI passes even though committed file was stale. Hmm, but hk fix stages affected files (per AGENTS.md "will stage affected files"), then `git diff --cached --exit-code` fails. So adding an hk fix step that regenerates would both fix locally (contributor runs `mise run fix` or pre-commit auto-fixes+stages) and fail CI on drift. 

But careful: pre-commit with fix=true auto-stages — meaning a contributor editing a manifest gets the workflow regenerated and staged automatically. That keeps workflow simple.

Also the generator tests: where to run them? Add a mise task `//:test-ci` or run within ci:lint? "test the generator" — I'll add unit tests `test_gen_ci_workflow.py` and a task `ci:test-workflow`? Hmm naming. Let me define:

- `[tasks."ci:workflow"]` — regenerate `.github/workflows/ci.yml` from `ci/targets/*.toml` (and validate variant coverage).
- `[tasks."ci:workflow-test"]`? Hmm. Or single task `ci:workflow` with usage spec arg `--check`. Mise TOML tasks can have usage. Simpler: two run entries:

```toml
[tasks."ci:workflow"]
description = "Regenerate the CI workflow from ci/targets manifests."
run = "uv run python .mise/scripts/generate_ci_workflow.py"

[tasks."ci:workflow-check"]? 
```

Hmm wait, actually maybe put the check into ci:lint list directly and tests into the hygiene job via ci:lint too? Tests for the generator could run as part of ci:lint: `uv run python -m unittest discover -s .mise/scripts -v`? That runs fast. But is lint the right place? The hygiene job runs ci:lint. Adding a check line there enforces sync in CI. For tests, maybe a separate task `ci:test-workflow` also run in hygiene job? Keep it simple: hygiene job steps: `mise run ci:lint`. I can add to ci:lint:

```toml
run = [
  "dprint output-resolved-config > /dev/null",
  "hk fix --all",
  "uv run python -m unittest discover ...", # generator tests
  "uv run python .mise/scripts/generate_ci_workflow.py --check",
  ...
]
```

Order: hk fix might reformat the generated workflow (dprint formats yaml?). Check dprint.jsonc. If dprint formats ci.yml, generator output must be dprint-clean. Let me check dprint config.

Also: the manifests themselves — dprint may format TOML. Generator output must survive `hk fix` (dprint + actionlint). I need to run the tools to verify. Environment: is mise installed in this container? Let me check `which mise` and whether tools are installed. There's `.venv` already. Let me test.

Plan of files:

1. `ci/targets/<variant>.toml` × 11 — manifests.
2. `.mise/scripts/generate_ci_workflow.py` — generator with `--check` mode.
3. `.mise/scripts/test_generate_ci_workflow.py` — unittest tests.
4. `mise.toml` — add tasks `ci:workflow` (regenerate), and integrate check+tests into `ci:lint`.
5. Regenerated `.github/workflows/ci.yml` (with generated header).
6. Maybe hk.pkl step to auto-regen on manifest edits? Optional; skip? "enforce that it stays in sync" — the ci:lint check enforces. Adding hk step makes contributor workflow simpler (auto-fix). Hmm, but hk step running `uv run python` on pre-commit adds latency. ci:lint check suffices for enforcement; contributor runs `mise run ci:workflow`. I'll also mention in AGENTS/docs? AGENTS.md says update docs if changing workflows mentioned. AGENTS.md mentions workflow commands generally, not CI internals. There's docs/src/content/docs/development/overview.md which describes tooling — maybe mentions CI. Let me check overview.md and CONTRIBUTING.md for CI mentions.

Also the manifest dir name: `ci/` at root. Alternatively `.github/ci/`. I'll use `ci/targets/` since it's contributor-facing policy, referenced from mise tasks.

Wait, one more consideration — generator determinism across platforms: run with `uv run python`, stdlib only (tomllib). Newlines: write with `\n` explicitly.

Let me check dprint.jsonc, overview.md, CONTRIBUTING.md, and whether tools are available in this environment.

# Contributing

Discuss changes in `#maplibre` on the
[OSM-US Slack](https://slack.openstreetmap.us/).

## Before Making Changes

See the
[development overview](https://maplibre.org/maplibre-native-ffi/development/)
for platform setup, pinned tools, local commands, tests, and examples.

Read [concepts](https://maplibre.org/maplibre-native-ffi/concepts/) before
changing behavior. Read the
[C API Conventions](https://maplibre.org/maplibre-native-ffi/development/c-conventions/)
before changing public C interfaces or C ABI behavior. Read the
[Binding specification](https://maplibre.org/maplibre-native-ffi/development/binding-specification/)
before changing language bindings or generated binding reference docs.

Keep pull requests focused on one reviewable change. The reviewer should be able
to connect the use case, public behavior, implementation, and validation without
separating unrelated work.

## Pull Requests

Open a pull request when the change is ready for review and include:

- the problem or use case;
- the public API or behavior change, if any;
- the validation you ran;
- platform limitations or native MapLibre behavior you checked;

If you use AI assistance, follow the [AI policy](./AI_POLICY.md).

## Before Making Changes

See the
[development overview](https://maplibre.org/maplibre-native-ffi/development/)
for platform setup, pinned tools, local commands, tests, and examples.

Read [concepts](https://maplibre.org/maplibre-native-ffi/concepts/) before

before changing language bindings or generated binding reference docs.

Keep pull requests focused on one reviewable change. The reviewer should be able

separating unrelated work.

toolchain inputs.

[`mise`](https://mise.jdx.dev/) is the contributor entrypoint. It pins top-level
tools, installs Git hooks, selects backend variants, and runs repository tasks.
Use `mise run ...` for common workflows: build, test, check, fix, and examples.
Mise also provides ecosystem entrypoints such as Pixi, Zig, Python, uv, Node,
and pnpm.

[`pixi`](https://pixi.sh/) installs the desktop native libraries used by local
builds. CMake, Ninja, pkg-config, shader tools, Doxygen, clang-format, and
clang-tidy are pinned by mise. Host platform toolchains provide C and C++
compilers, and CMake builds the native C/C++ library.

Language package managers own dependencies inside their ecosystems. For example,
`uv` owns Python package dependencies, `pnpm` owns Node package dependencies,
Gradle owns Java and Kotlin dependencies, and Cargo owns Rust dependencies.
Language-specific formatters, linters, analyzers, test frameworks, and code
generators usually live with the language package graph they serve.

[`hk`](https://github.com/jdx/hk) orchestrates repository checks for pre-commit,
`mise run check`, and `mise run fix`. [`dprint`](https://dprint.dev/) owns
repository-wide formatting defaults.

[Astro](https://astro.build/) and [Starlight](https://starlight.astro.build/)
build the documentation site. Generated API reference HTML is installed into
`docs/public/reference/` before each docs build.

Every feature needs automated CI coverage when practical. The root
`mise run test` command builds the native library, runs the direct Zig C API
suite, and runs the public Zig binding suite for supported host variants.

Use examples for demos and behavior that needs manual validation, such as visual
output, interactive input, or host graphics integration.

Keep examples small. This repository includes low-level language bindings and
focused integration examples. Full application SDKs live outside this
repository.

Now design final structure:

**Manifests** — `ci/targets/<variant>.toml`, one per variant, 11 files. Schema:

```toml
# CI target manifest for the linux-x64-egl variant.
# Regenerate .github/workflows/ci.yml after editing: mise run ci:workflow

# GitHub Actions runner for this variant.
runner = "ubuntu-latest"

# run = build and test natively; build = build only (no display/driver to test)
mode = "test"

# CI steps enabled on this variant. Omit a step or set enabled = false to skip.
[steps.kotlin-native]
enabled = false
reason = "no Kotlin/Native linux-arm64 host — https://youtrack.jetbrains.com/issue/KT-36871"
```

Hmm, but most steps default enabled. Where do defaults live? Policy should be declarative and explicit. Explicitly listing all 12 steps in each manifest is verbose but maximally declarative. Middle ground: generator defines step defaults as "enabled", manifests override. But then policy is split. Hmm.

Actually, let me think about what's cleanest. The steps with their properties:

```
steps (in order):
1. java-ffm:   name "Build Java FFM binding", run "mise run //bindings/java-ffm:build"
2. java-jni:   name "Build Java JNI binding", run "mise run //bindings/java-jni:build"
3. dotnet:     name "Test .NET binding", run "mise run //bindings/dotnet:test"
4. kotlin-native: name "Test Kotlin/Native binding", run "mise run //bindings/kotlin-native:build"
5. rust:       name "Test Rust binding", run "mise run //bindings/rust:ci"
6. swift:      name "Test Swift binding", run "mise run //bindings/swift:test"
7. zig:        name "Test Zig binding", run "mise run //bindings/zig:ci"
8. lwjgl:      name "Build LWJGL example", run "mise run //examples/lwjgl-map:build"
9. rust-example: name "Test Rust example", run "mise run //examples/rust-map:ci"
10. swift-example: name "Build Swift example", run "mise run //examples/swift-map:build"
11. zig-example: name "Build Zig example", run "mise run //examples/zig-map:build"
12. zig-readback: name "Run Zig readback example", run "mise run //examples/zig-readback:run"
```

Step identity/metadata (name, command, order) is workflow structure → belongs in the generator. Per-target enablement is policy → manifests. For defaults, I'll make manifests fully explicit per step with simple booleans, using TOML inline tables only when a reason is needed:

```toml
[steps]
java-ffm = true
java-jni = true
dotnet = true
kotlin-native = { enabled = false, reason = "..." }
rust = true
swift = false
zig = true
lwjgl = false
rust-example = true
swift-example = false
zig-example = true
zig-readback = true
```

Fully explicit, declarative, readable. Generator validates: every manifest lists exactly the known step keys; every .mise/config.*.toml variant has a manifest and vice versa; runner non-empty; mode in {test, build}.

Mode: `test = true/false` boolean. Name: `test = true` reads well ("run tests on this target"). ios manifests: `test = false`.

Wait, but also with test=false, all steps are false for ios. Fine.

**Reasons → comments**: For each step, collect (variant, reason) where disabled with reason. Emit above the step as comments, e.g.:

```yaml
      # linux-arm64: no Kotlin/Native host — https://youtrack.jetbrains.com/issue/KT-36871
      # windows: no MSVC target — https://youtrack.jetbrains.com/issue/SKIKO-446
      - name: Test Kotlin/Native binding
```

Current comments use platform prefixes ("linux-arm64", "windows") not full variant names. To keep it simple, emit reasons per variant that has one:

```
# Disabled on linux-arm64-egl, linux-arm64-vulkan: no Kotlin/Native host — ...
# Disabled on windows-x64-vulkan, windows-x64-wgl: no MSVC target — ...
```

Group variants by identical reason string. That's clean and preserves the information (even improves precision).

**Condition generation**: For each step, enabled set E over all variants V.
- If E == V: no `if`.
- Else: `if: ${{ contains(fromJSON('["a","b"]'), matrix.mise_env) }}` with enabled variants sorted? Order: use manifest sort order (sorted variant names, matching current matrix ordering which is alphabetical). Current matrix order is alphabetical: linux-arm64-egl, linux-arm64-vulkan, linux-x64-egl, linux-x64-vulkan, macos-arm64-egl... wait current: macos-arm64-metal, macos-arm64-vulkan, macos-arm64-egl — that's NOT alphabetical (egl < metal < vulkan alphabetically). Current order: metal, vulkan, egl. Hmm. And ios-arm64-metal, ios-simulator-arm64-metal, windows-x64-vulkan, windows-x64-wgl. So mostly grouped by platform with hand ordering. For generated file, I'll sort alphabetically for determinism — "preserve behavior" (set membership identical), ordering irrelevant to behavior. Alphabetical: ios-arm64-metal, ios-simulator-arm64-metal, linux-arm64-egl, linux-arm64-vulkan, linux-x64-egl, linux-x64-vulkan, macos-arm64-egl, macos-arm64-metal, macos-arm64-vulkan, windows-x64-vulkan, windows-x64-wgl. That's fine and deterministic.

Also with fromJSON the check is positive membership — good "positive wording" style.

**Generated workflow structure**: The generator contains a template for the fixed parts (header, on, permissions, concurrency, hygiene, docs, variants job skeleton, required job) and injects: matrix include list, native build/test steps, binding/example steps with ifs. Everything else byte-identical to current.

Generated header comment at top:

```yaml
# Generated by .mise/scripts/generate_ci_workflow.py from ci/targets/*.toml.
# Do not edit by hand; run `mise run ci:workflow` to regenerate.
```

**Naming of tasks**:
- `mise run ci:workflow` — regenerate.
- check integrated into `ci:lint` run list: `uv run python .mise/scripts/generate_ci_workflow.py --check` and `uv run python -m unittest ...`.

Wait — should tests be part of ci:lint? ci:lint is "lint". Maybe add separate task `ci:workflow-test` and call both in the hygiene job? But hygiene job just runs `mise run ci:lint`, which is generated into the workflow... The hygiene/docs jobs are part of the template — I could add a step in hygiene: `run: mise run ci:workflow-test`? Hmm. Simplest: keep the hygiene job steps identical (`mise run ci:lint`) and add to ci:lint's run list:

```toml
[tasks."ci:lint"]
run = [
  "dprint output-resolved-config > /dev/null",
  "hk fix --all",
  "git update-index -q --refresh",
  "uv run python -m unittest discover -s .mise/scripts -p 'test_*.py' -q",

  'git diff --exit-code -- . ":(exclude)mise.lock"',

```

Hmm wait, ordering: hk fix runs formatters on ci.yml; then generator --check compares generator output to the (formatted) file. If dprint's pretty_yaml would reformat generator output, --check fails. Since I can't run dprint here, I must emit formatting identical to what pretty_yaml produces. The existing ci.yml is dprint-formatted; I'll mirror its style exactly. Inline tables `{ enabled = false, reason = "..." }` — dprint toml formats inline tables... can't verify. Keep manifests simple to minimize risk: maybe avoid inline tables? Alternative for reasons: separate table:

```toml
[steps]
java-ffm = true
...

[reasons]
kotlin-native = "no Kotlin/Native host for linux-arm64 — https://..."
```

That's clean plain TOML. `steps` values all booleans; `reasons` optional map of step → reason (only meaningful for disabled steps; generator validates reasons reference disabled steps... or just known steps). I like this.

Manifest schema final:

```toml
# linux-arm64-egl.toml
runner = "ubuntu-24.04-arm"
test = true

[steps]
dotnet = true
java-ffm = true
java-jni = true
kotlin-native = false
lwjgl = false
rust = true

```

For ios manifests: `test = false` and all steps false. And comment explaining build-only.

Should `test=false` even allow steps=true? Generator validates: if test=false then all steps must be false? Currently yes (ios has everything off). Enforce to keep policy coherent? That might be over-validation; but it's a sensible invariant: steps require the test toolchain. Hmm — not necessarily; a build-only target could still build bindings. Actually bindings build requires native build only. I'll not enforce; just allow.

**Matrix runner**: from manifest `runner`.

**Generator script** `.mise/scripts/generate_ci_workflow.py`:

- Load all `ci/targets/*.toml` via tomllib.
- Validate schema: known keys (runner, test, steps, reasons), runner str, test bool, steps exactly the known step set (all 12 keys present, bool values), reasons ⊆ steps with enabled=false.

Template: build the YAML as text with the fixed parts verbatim. I'll write the template carefully.

Actually simpler determinism: always list enabled targets positively — fully declarative mirror of manifests. Long lines but unambiguous. Hmm.
```python
def _condition(enabled: list[str], variants: list[str]) -> str | None:
    if not enabled:
        raise ... # caller ensures nonempty
    if len(enabled) == len(variants):
        return None
    disabled = [v for v in variants if v not in enabled]
    if len(enabled) <= len(disabled):
        listed, negate = enabled, False
    else:
        listed, negate = disabled, True
    members = json.dumps(listed, separators=(",", ":"))
    op = "!" if negate else ""
    return f"${{{{ {op}contains(fromJSON('{members}'), matrix.mise_env) }}}}"
```

input.content:
"""Generate .github/workflows/ci.yml from the CI target manifests.

The CI target support policy is declared in ci/targets/<variant>.toml, one
manifest per mise variant. This script renders the checked-in GitHub Actions
workflow from those manifests so the policy has a single declarative source.

Run `mise run ci:workflow` to regenerate the workflow after editing a
manifest. `mise run ci:lint` runs this script with --check and fails when the
checked-in workflow drifts from the manifests.
"""

from __future__ import annotations

import argparse
import difflib
import json
import sys
import tomllib
from dataclasses import dataclass
from pathlib import Path

REPO_ROOT = Path(__file__).resolve().parents[2]
TARGETS_DIR = REPO_ROOT / "ci" / "targets"
MISE_DIR = REPO_ROOT / ".mise"
WORKFLOW_PATH = REPO_ROOT / ".github" / "workflows" / "ci.yml"

CHECKOUT = (
    "- uses: actions/checkout@df4cb1c069e1874edd31b4311f1884172cec0e10 # v6.0.3"
)


class ManifestError(Exception):
    """A target manifest is missing, malformed, or inconsistent."""


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class Step:
    """A binding or example step of the variants job."""

    key: str
    name: str
    run: str


# Step order matches the workflow: bindings first, then examples.
STEPS = (
    Step("java-ffm", "Build Java FFM binding", "mise run //bindings/java-ffm:build"),
    Step("java-jni", "Build Java JNI binding", "mise run //bindings/java-jni:build"),
    Step("dotnet", "Test .NET binding", "mise run //bindings/dotnet:test"),
    Step(
        "kotlin-native",
        "Test Kotlin/Native binding",
        "mise run //bindings/kotlin-native:build",
    ),
    Step("rust", "Test Rust binding", "mise run //bindings/rust:ci"),
    Step("swift", "Test Swift binding", "mise run //bindings/swift:test"),
    Step("zig", "Test Zig binding", "mise run //bindings/zig:ci"),
    Step("lwjgl", "Build LWJGL example", "mise run //examples/lwjgl-map:build"),
    Step("rust-example", "Test Rust example", "mise run //examples/rust-map:ci"),
    Step(
        "swift-example", "Build Swift example", "mise run //examples/swift-map:build"
    ),
    Step("zig-example", "Build Zig example", "mise run //examples/zig-map:build"),
    Step(
        "zig-readback",
        "Run Zig readback example",
        "mise run //examples/zig-readback:run",
    ),
)

STEP_KEYS = frozenset(step.key for step in STEPS)


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class Target:
    """The CI policy for one mise variant."""

    variant: str
    runner: str
    test: bool
    steps: dict[str, bool]
    reasons: dict[str, str]


def _load_target(path: Path) -> Target:
    data = tomllib.loads(path.read_text(encoding="utf-8"))

    unknown_keys = sorted(set(data) - {"runner", "test", "steps", "reasons"})
    if unknown_keys:
        raise ManifestError(f"{path}: unknown keys: {', '.join(unknown_keys)}")

    runner = data.get("runner")
    if not isinstance(runner, str) or not runner:
        raise ManifestError(f"{path}: 'runner' must be a non-empty string")

    test = data.get("test")
    if not isinstance(test, bool):
        raise ManifestError(f"{path}: 'test' must be a boolean")

    steps = data.get("steps")
    if not isinstance(steps, dict):
        raise ManifestError(f"{path}: [steps] table is required")
    unknown_steps = sorted(set(steps) - STEP_KEYS)
    if unknown_steps:
        raise ManifestError(f"{path}: unknown steps: {', '.join(unknown_steps)}")
    missing_steps = sorted(STEP_KEYS - set(steps))
    if missing_steps:
        raise ManifestError(
            f"{path}: missing steps (list every step): {', '.join(missing_steps)}"
        )
    for key, enabled in steps.items():
        if not isinstance(enabled, bool):
            raise ManifestError(f"{path}: steps.{key} must be a boolean")

    reasons = data.get("reasons", {})
    if not isinstance(reasons, dict):
        raise ManifestError(f"{path}: [reasons] must be a table")
    for key, reason in reasons.items():
        if key not in STEP_KEYS:
            raise ManifestError(f"{path}: reasons.{key} is not a known step")
        if not isinstance(reason, str) or not reason:
            raise ManifestError(f"{path}: reasons.{key} must be a non-empty string")
        if steps.get(key):
            raise ManifestError(
                f"{path}: reasons.{key} documents a skipped step, "
                f"but steps.{key} is true"
            )

    return Target(path.stem, runner, test, dict(steps), dict(reasons))


def load_targets(targets_dir: Path) -> dict[str, Target]:
    """Load and validate every manifest in targets_dir, keyed by variant."""
    targets = {
        path.stem: _load_target(path)
        for path in sorted(targets_dir.glob("*.toml"))
    }
    if not targets:
        raise ManifestError(f"no target manifests found in {targets_dir}")
    return targets


def validate_targets(targets: dict[str, Target], mise_dir: Path) -> None:
    """Check cross-cutting invariants between manifests and mise variants."""
    mise_variants = sorted(
        path.name.removeprefix("config.").removesuffix(".toml")
        for path in mise_dir.glob("config.*.toml")
    )
    missing = sorted(set(mise_variants) - set(targets))
    extra = sorted(set(targets) - set(mise_variants))
    problems = []
    if missing:
        problems.append(f"mise variants without a manifest: {', '.join(missing)}")
    if extra:
        problems.append(f"manifests without a mise variant: {', '.join(extra)}")
    if problems:
        raise ManifestError("; ".join(problems))

    for step in STEPS:
        if not any(target.steps[step.key] for target in targets.values()):
            raise ManifestError(
                f"step '{step.key}' is not enabled on any target; "
                "remove it from the generator or enable it in a manifest"
            )


def _condition(enabled: list[str], variants: list[str]) -> str | None:
    """Render a matrix `if` expression for the targets where a step runs.

    Lists the smaller side of enabled/disabled so the workflow stays readable;
    returns None when the step runs on every target.
    """
    if not enabled:
        raise ManifestError("cannot render a condition for a step with no targets")
    if len(enabled) == len(variants):
        return None
    disabled = [variant for variant in variants if variant not in enabled]
    if len(enabled) <= len(disabled):
        listed, negate = enabled, ""
    else:
        listed, negate = disabled, "!"
    members = json.dumps(listed, separators=(",", ":"))
    expr = f"{negate}contains(fromJSON('{members}'), matrix.mise_env)"
    return "${{ " + expr + " }}"


def _reason_comments(step_key: str, targets: dict[str, Target]) -> list[str]:
    """Group skip reasons by text and render them as workflow comments."""
    groups: dict[str, list[str]] = {}
    for variant in sorted(targets):
        reason = targets[variant].reasons.get(step_key)
        if reason is not None:
            groups.setdefault(reason, []).append(variant)
    return [
        f"      # Disabled on {', '.join(variants)}: {reason}"
        for reason, variants in groups.items()
    ]


def _render_named_step(
    lines: list[str],
    name: str,
    run: str,
    enabled: list[str],
    variants: list[str],
) -> None:
    lines.append(f"      - name: {name}")

    if condition is not None:
        lines.append(f"        if: {condition}")
    lines.append(f"        run: {run}")

# Generated from ci/targets/*.toml by .mise/scripts/generate_ci_workflow.py.
# Edit the manifests and run `mise run ci:workflow`; do not edit this file.
name: CI

on:
  push:
    branches: [main]
  pull_request:
    branches: [main]

permissions:
  contents: read

concurrency:
  group: ${{ github.workflow }}-${{ github.event.pull_request.number || github.ref }}
  cancel-in-progress: ${{ github.event_name == 'pull_request' }}

jobs:
  hygiene:
    name: hygiene
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    timeout-minutes: 30
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@df4cb1c069e1874edd31b4311f1884172cec0e10 # v6.0.3
      - uses: ./.github/actions/setup-ci-deps
      - run: mise run ci:lint

  docs:
    name: docs
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    timeout-minutes: 60
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@df4cb1c069e1874edd31b4311f1884172cec0e10 # v6.0.3
      - uses: ./.github/actions/setup-ci-deps
      - run: mise run //docs:build

  variants:
    name: variant / ${{ matrix.mise_env }}
    runs-on: ${{ matrix.runner }}
    timeout-minutes: 120
    env:
      MISE_ENV: ${{ matrix.mise_env }}
    strategy:
      fail-fast: false
      matrix:
        include:
"""

_FOOTER = """\

  required:
    name: ci-required
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    timeout-minutes: 5
    if: ${{ always() }}
    needs:
      - hygiene
      - docs
      - variants
    steps:
      - name: Fail if required jobs failed
        if: ${{ contains(needs.*.result, 'failure') || contains(needs.*.result, 'cancelled') || contains(needs.*.result, 'skipped') }}
        run: |
          echo '${{ toJSON(needs) }}'
          exit 1

      - run: true
"""


def render(targets: dict[str, Target]) -> str:
    """Render the full CI workflow from validated target manifests."""
    variants = sorted(targets)
    parts = [_HEADER]

    for variant in variants:
        parts.append(f"          - mise_env: {variant}\n")
        parts.append(f"            runner: {targets[variant].runner}\n")

    parts.append("\n    steps:\n")
    parts.append(f"      {CHECKOUT}\n")
    parts.append("      - uses: ./.github/actions/setup-ci-deps\n")
    parts.append("\n      - run: mise run configure\n")

    lines: list[str] = []
    build_only = [variant for variant in variants if not targets[variant].test]
    tested = [variant for variant in variants if targets[variant].test]
    if build_only:
        _render_named_step(
            lines, "Build native library", "mise run build", build_only, variants
        )
    _render_named_step(
        lines, "Test native library", "mise run test", tested, variants
    )

    lines.append("")
    for index, step in enumerate(STEPS):
        if index:
            lines.append("") if step.key == "lwjgl" else None
        lines.extend(_reason_comments(step.key, targets))
        enabled = [
            variant for variant in variants if targets[variant].steps[step.key]
        ]
        _render_named_step(lines, step.name, step.run, enabled, variants)

    parts.append("\n".join(lines))
    parts.append(_FOOTER)
    return "".join(parts)


def main(argv: list[str] | None = None) -> int:
    parser = argparse.ArgumentParser(description=__doc__)
    parser.add_argument(
        "--check",
        action="store_true",
        help="fail if the checked-in workflow differs from the manifests",
    )
    args = parser.parse_args(argv)

    try:
        targets = load_targets(TARGETS_DIR)
        validate_targets(targets, MISE_DIR)
        rendered = render(targets)
    except ManifestError as error:
        print(f"error: {error}", file=sys.stderr)
        return 1

    relative_path = WORKFLOW_PATH.relative_to(REPO_ROOT)
    if args.check:
        current = WORKFLOW_PATH.read_text(encoding="utf-8")
        if current != rendered:
            diff = difflib.unified_diff(
                current.splitlines(keepends=True),
                rendered.splitlines(keepends=True),
                fromfile=str(relative_path),
                tofile=f"{relative_path} (generated)",
            )
            sys.stdout.writelines(diff)
            print(
                f"\n{relative_path} is out of sync with ci/targets/*.toml; "
                "run `mise run ci:workflow` to regenerate it.",
                file=sys.stderr,
            )
            return 1
        return 0

    WORKFLOW_PATH.write_text(rendered, encoding="utf-8", newline="\n")
    print(f"generated {relative_path} from {len(targets)} target manifests")
    return 0


if __name__ == "__main__":
    raise SystemExit(main())

"""Tests for the CI workflow generator.

Run with `uv run python -m unittest discover -s .mise/scripts` from the
repository root (also covered by `mise run ci:lint`).
"""

import tempfile
import unittest
from pathlib import Path

import generate_ci_workflow as gen


def _manifest_steps(**overrides: bool) -> str:
    steps = dict.fromkeys(gen.STEP_KEYS, True)
    steps.update(overrides)
    return "\n".join(
        f"{key} = {str(enabled).lower()}" for key, enabled in sorted(steps.items())
    )


def _write_manifest(
    directory: Path,
    variant: str,
    *,
    runner: str = "ubuntu-latest",
    test: bool = True,
    steps: str | None = None,
    extra: str = "",
) -> None:
    body = f'runner = "{runner}"\ntest = {str(test).lower()}\n'
    body += f"\n[steps]\n{steps if steps is not None else _manifest_steps()}\n"
    body += extra
    (directory / f"{variant}.toml").write_text(body, encoding="utf-8")


class ManifestTest(unittest.TestCase):
    def test_manifests_match_mise_variants(self) -> None:
        targets = gen.load_targets(gen.TARGETS_DIR)
        gen.validate_targets(targets, gen.MISE_DIR)
        self.assertGreaterEqual(len(targets), 2)

    def test_every_manifest_lists_every_step(self) -> None:
        for variant, target in gen.load_targets(gen.TARGETS_DIR).items():
            self.assertEqual(set(target.steps), gen.STEP_KEYS, variant)

    def test_rejects_unknown_step(self) -> None:
        with tempfile.TemporaryDirectory() as tmp:
            _write_manifest(Path(tmp), "linux-x64-egl", extra="\nunknown = true\n")
            with self.assertRaises(gen.ManifestError):
                gen.load_targets(Path(tmp))

    def test_rejects_missing_step(self) -> None:
        with tempfile.TemporaryDirectory() as tmp:
            steps = _manifest_steps().replace("zig = true\n", "")
            _write_manifest(Path(tmp), "linux-x64-egl", steps=steps)
            with self.assertRaises(gen.ManifestError):
                gen.load_targets(Path(tmp))

    def test_rejects_non_boolean_step(self) -> None:
        with tempfile.TemporaryDirectory() as tmp:
            steps = _manifest_steps().replace("zig = true", 'zig = "yes"')
            _write_manifest(Path(tmp), "linux-x64-egl", steps=steps)
            with self.assertRaises(gen.ManifestError):
                gen.load_targets(Path(tmp))

    def test_rejects_reason_for_enabled_step(self) -> None:
        with tempfile.TemporaryDirectory() as tmp:
            _write_manifest(
                Path(tmp),
                "linux-x64-egl",
                extra='\n[reasons]\nzig = "not skipped"\n',
            )
            with self.assertRaises(gen.ManifestError):
                gen.load_targets(Path(tmp))

    def test_rejects_missing_manifest_for_mise_variant(self) -> None:
        targets = gen.load_targets(gen.TARGETS_DIR)
        del targets[sorted(targets)[0]]
        with self.assertRaises(gen.ManifestError):
            gen.validate_targets(targets, gen.MISE_DIR)

    def test_rejects_step_enabled_nowhere(self) -> None:
        targets = gen.load_targets(gen.TARGETS_DIR)
        disabled = {
            variant: gen.Target(
                target.variant,
                target.runner,
                target.test,
                {**target.steps, "zig": False},
                target.reasons,
            )
            for variant, target in targets.items()
        }
        with self.assertRaises(gen.ManifestError):
            gen.validate_targets(disabled, gen.MISE_DIR)


class ConditionTest(unittest.TestCase):
    VARIANTS = ["a", "b", "c", "d", "e"]

    def test_no_condition_when_enabled_everywhere(self) -> None:
        self.assertIsNone(gen._condition(self.VARIANTS, self.VARIANTS))

    def test_lists_enabled_targets_when_fewer(self) -> None:
        condition = gen._condition(["a", "b"], self.VARIANTS)
        self.assertEqual(
            condition,
            "${{ contains(fromJSON('[\"a\",\"b\"]'), matrix.mise_env) }}",
        )

    def test_lists_disabled_targets_when_fewer(self) -> None:
        condition = gen._condition(["a", "b", "c", "d"], self.VARIANTS)
        self.assertEqual(
            condition,
            "${{ !contains(fromJSON('[\"e\"]'), matrix.mise_env) }}",
        )

    def test_rejects_empty_enabled_set(self) -> None:
        with self.assertRaises(gen.ManifestError):
            gen._condition([], self.VARIANTS)


class RenderTest(unittest.TestCase):
    def setUp(self) -> None:
        self.targets = gen.load_targets(gen.TARGETS_DIR)

    def test_checked_in_workflow_is_in_sync(self) -> None:
        rendered = gen.render(self.targets)
        checked_in = gen.WORKFLOW_PATH.read_text(encoding="utf-8")
        self.assertEqual(
            checked_in,
            rendered,
            "workflow drifted; run `mise run ci:workflow` to regenerate",
        )

    def test_render_is_deterministic(self) -> None:
        self.assertEqual(gen.render(self.targets), gen.render(self.targets))

    def test_matrix_contains_every_manifest_runner(self) -> None:
        rendered = gen.render(self.targets)
        for variant, target in self.targets.items():
            self.assertIn(f"- mise_env: {variant}\n", rendered)
            self.assertIn(f"runner: {target.runner}\n", rendered)

    def test_reason_comments_group_variants_by_reason(self) -> None:
        comments = gen._reason_comments("kotlin-native", self.targets)
        self.assertTrue(comments)
        for comment in comments:
            self.assertTrue(comment.startswith("      # Disabled on "))
        commented = ", ".join(
            comment.removeprefix("      # Disabled on ").split(":")[0]
            for comment in comments
        )
        for variant, target in self.targets.items():
            has_reason = "kotlin-native" in target.reasons
            self.assertEqual(variant in commented, has_reason, variant)


if __name__ == "__main__":
    unittest.main()

display.text:
Mise selects the native build profile that matches your host when one is
available. Set `MISE_ENV=<variant>` before `mise run ...`, or pass
`mise -E <variant> run ...`, to build, test, or run examples for another
platform and render backend.

## Common Commands

```bash
# Build and test the C API (via Zig bindings)
mise run test

# Build only
mise run build

# Run linters and formatters
mise run fix

# Run examples
mise run //examples/zig-map:run

# Build the documentation site
mise run //docs:build
```

## How Tools Fit Together

This repository spans native code, language bindings, examples, tests, and
documentation. Each tool owns the layer where it has the clearest dependency
model.

Project-managed dependency state keeps builds reproducible. Mise supplies
repository tools, owns task execution, and exports the environment consumed by
build systems, bindings, and examples. Pixi currently supplies desktop native
libraries from [`conda-forge`](https://conda-forge.org/). Platform SDKs such as
Xcode, Visual Studio, Linux compiler packages, and the Android SDK remain host
toolchain inputs.

[`mise`](https://mise.jdx.dev/) is the contributor entrypoint. It pins top-level
tools, installs Git hooks, selects backend variants, and runs repository tasks.
Use `mise run ...` for common workflows: build, test, check, fix, and examples.
Mise also provides ecosystem entrypoints such as Pixi, Zig, Python, uv, Node,
and pnpm.

[`pixi`](https://pixi.sh/) installs the desktop native libraries used by local
builds. CMake, Ninja, pkg-config, shader tools, Doxygen, clang-format, and

Content (following prose guidelines, positive wording, textWrap always at 80 cols):

```markdown
## Continuous Integration

The CI target support policy lives in `ci/targets/<variant>.toml`, one
manifest per mise variant. Each manifest declares the GitHub Actions runner,
whether the native test suite runs (build-only targets such as iOS set
`test = false`), and the binding and example steps enabled on that target,
with optional reasons for skipped steps.

`.github/workflows/ci.yml` is generated from these manifests and checked in.
Run `mise run ci:workflow` after editing a manifest. `mise run ci:lint` tests
the generator and fails when the checked-in workflow drifts from the
manifests.
```

AGENTS.md workflow block addition:

```bash
# Regenerate the CI workflow after editing ci/targets/*.toml
mise run ci:workflow
```

- `ci/` — Declarative CI target manifests from which the GitHub Actions workflow
  is generated.

And maybe note in workflow section. Keep minimal.

part.text: Done. Summarize concisely.

Done and committed (`c0da4a5`). Summary:

**Declarative policy** — `ci/targets/<variant>.toml`, one manifest per CI variant (11 total). Each declares the runner, `test = true/false` (iOS is build-only), the exact set of enabled binding/example steps, and optional skip reasons (e.g. Kotlin/Native on linux-arm64/windows, with the YouTrack links from the old hand-written comments).

**Generator** — `.mise/scripts/generate_ci_workflow.py` (stdlib-only, run via `uv run`) renders the checked-in `.github/workflows/ci.yml`. It validates manifests strictly (unknown/missing steps, reasons only on skipped steps), cross-checks that manifests and `.mise/config.*.toml` variants stay in one-to-one sync, and emits per-step `if:` conditions as explicit target-membership expressions (listing the smaller of enabled/disabled), with skip reasons rendered as workflow comments.

**Sync enforcement** — `mise run ci:workflow` regenerates; `mise run ci:lint` (the CI hygiene job) now runs the generator's 16 unit tests and `--check`, which fails with a diff when the workflow drifts. Ruff and `ty` already cover `.mise` scripts, so the generator and tests are linted/type-checked by the existing hk hooks.

**Behavior preserved** — I verified equivalence with a script that evaluated every step condition from the old and new workflows across all 11 variants: 14 steps × 11 variants identical, same runners, same commands. The only deliberate diffs are alphabetical matrix ordering, the generated-header comment, and the duplicated-policy conditions rewritten as membership lists. A manifest round-trip (flip `zig-example` on macos-arm64-egl) confirmed edits propagate correctly.

Docs updated: new "Continuous Integration" section in `docs/.../overview.md` and the `ci/targets/` + `ci:workflow` entries in AGENTS.md. One caveat: dprint/actionlint aren't installed in this sandbox, so `hk fix` couldn't be run locally — the generated YAML deliberately mirrors the previously dprint-formatted file's style, and CI's hygiene job will confirm.
